## Freelance Services Agreement (Brand Refresh 2025)

### Preamble

This Freelance Services Agreement ("Agreement") is made and entered into on 2025-01-15 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., a California corporation, acting by and through Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, acting by and through Alex Chen, Senior Designer ("Freelancer"). Client and Freelancer are each a "Party" and together the "Parties." The Parties agree that Freelancer will perform the services described below for the Project "Brand Refresh 2025" under the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement.

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to perform a complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document (the "Project"); and

WHEREAS, Freelancer represents that Freelancer has the skill, experience, and qualifications to perform the services required for the Project and agrees to provide such services under the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

---

## Clauses (23 total)

### Scope of Services

*Type: standard*

Freelancer shall design and deliver the following deliverables for the Project "Brand Refresh 2025":

- Logo concepts (3 options) — due January 30, 2025 — Fee: $2,000
- Selected logo refinement — due February 15, 2025 — Fee: $1,500
- Brand guidelines document — due March 1, 2025 — Fee: $2,500
- Asset package delivery (final file package: vector and raster files, web-optimized assets, color and typography files) — due March 15, 2025 — Fee: $2,500

Freelancer will provide up to three (3) rounds of revisions for each deliverable as part of the fees set forth. Additional revisions requested by Client will be billed in accordance with the rate set forth in Clause 10 (Additional Work and Change Orders). The Parties agree that the Deliverables, due dates, and fees are a part of this Agreement. Freelancer shall perform services in a professional and timely manner consistent with industry standards.

### Payment; Fees; Deposit

*Type: standard*

Total Fee: $8,500. A non-refundable deposit of $2,550 (30% of Total Fee) is due upon execution of this Agreement and must be paid before Freelancer will commence work. The remaining balance of $5,950 shall be paid according to the milestone schedule below. All amounts are stated in U.S. dollars.

Payment Schedule (milestone):
- Deposit due on signing (non-refundable except as stated below): $2,550
- Upon delivery of Logo concepts (due Jan 30, 2025): $2,000 (minus any deposit applied)
- Upon delivery of Selected logo refinement (due Feb 15, 2025): $1,500
- Upon delivery of Brand guidelines document (due Mar 1, 2025): $2,500
- Upon delivery of Asset package (due Mar 15, 2025): $2,500

Client shall pay invoices within fourteen (14) calendar days of receipt. Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month (18% per annum) or the highest rate permitted by law, whichever is lower. Freelancer may suspend work if Client fails to pay any undisputed invoice within seven (7) days after receipt of written notice of non-payment.

### Acceptance; Review Period

*Type: standard*

For each deliverable, Client shall have seven (7) business days from receipt to review and either (a) provide written acceptance, or (b) provide written comments requesting revisions. If Client fails to provide timely written acceptance or comments within the seven (7) business day review period, the deliverable will be deemed accepted and Freelancer may invoice for the applicable milestone payment. Acceptance shall not relieve Client of its obligation to pay any outstanding fees.

### Revisions

*Type: standard*

Freelancer will include up to three (3) rounds of revisions for each deliverable at no additional cost. A "round" of revision is defined as a single set of consolidated comments submitted by Client. Additional revision rounds requested by Client beyond the three (3) included rounds will be billed at Freelancer's hourly rate of $120 per hour, or at a fixed fee agreed in writing by the Parties. Substantive scope changes (e.g., new creative direction or additional deliverables) shall be treated as Change Orders under Clause 10.

### Timeline and Deadline

*Type: standard*

Freelancer shall use commercially reasonable efforts to meet the project deadlines set forth in Clause 1.0. The Project Deadline for completion of all Deliverables is March 15, 2025. Delivery dates assume timely feedback and approvals from Client. Freelancer is not responsible for delays caused by Client's failure to provide materials, approvals, or timely feedback. Any revisions to the timeline must be agreed to in writing by both Parties.

### Client Responsibilities

*Type: standard*

Client shall provide Freelancer with timely access to Client personnel, materials, brand assets, factual information, and approvals necessary for Freelancer to perform the services. Client is responsible for the accuracy of materials provided to Freelancer. Freelancer shall not be responsible for defects in materials provided by Client or for delays attributable to Client's failure to cooperate.

### Ownership and Intellectual Property

*Type: standard*

Upon full payment of all fees and expenses owed under this Agreement, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title and interest in and to the final Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement, excluding Freelancer's pre-existing materials, processes, templates, concepts and tools ("Freelancer Materials"), which remain the sole property of Freelancer. Freelancer hereby grants Client a perpetual, worldwide, exclusive, transferable license to use the Freelancer Materials only to the extent incorporated in the final Deliverables. Freelancer retains the right to display the Deliverables and samples of work in Freelancer's portfolio and marketing materials, provided such display does not disclose Client's confidential information.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

Each Party agrees to keep confidential all non-public information of the other Party disclosed in connection with the Project ("Confidential Information") and to use such information only for the purposes of performing under this Agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that is (a) or becomes publicly known through no breach by the receiving Party, (b) is already known by the receiving Party without restriction, (c) received from a third party without breach of any obligation of confidentiality, or (d) independently developed by the receiving Party. The confidentiality obligations survive termination of this Agreement for a period of three (3) years, except for trade secrets which are protected for as long as they remain trade secrets under law.

### Representations and Warranties

*Type: standard*

Freelancer represents that (a) Freelancer has the right and authority to enter into this Agreement and to grant the rights granted herein; and (b) to Freelancer's knowledge, the Deliverables will not infringe any third party's intellectual property rights when used in accordance with this Agreement. Client represents that it has the right to provide to Freelancer any materials provided and that such materials will not infringe third party rights. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS CLAUSE, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

### Change Orders; Additional Work

*Type: standard*

Any changes to the Scope of Services, Deliverables, schedule or fees shall be made only by a written change order signed by both Parties (a "Change Order"). Additional services beyond the Scope of Services will be billed at the hourly rate of $120 per hour or at such other rate as the Parties may agree in writing. Change Orders may affect the schedule and fees; Freelancer is not obligated to commence extra work until the Parties agree in writing on any additional fees and schedule adjustments.

### Termination

*Type: standard*

Either Party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other Party if the breaching Party fails to cure such breach within ten (10) days of receipt of written notice specifying the breach. Client may terminate for convenience upon fifteen (15) days' written notice. In the event of termination by Client for convenience, Freelancer shall be entitled to payment for work performed through the effective date of termination (calculated on a pro rata basis) plus reimbursement of any non-cancelable expenses; the deposit shall be applied against such amounts. If Client has paid more than the earned amount, Freelancer shall refund the unearned portion within thirty (30) days. If Freelancer terminates other than for Client's breach, Freelancer shall refund any unearned portion of prepaid fees, including the deposit, within thirty (30) days; provided that Freelancer may retain payment for work actually performed. Termination does not relieve Client of the obligation to pay for accepted deliverables.

### Indemnification

*Type: standard*

Each Party ("Indemnifying Party") shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party and its officers, directors, employees and agents ("Indemnified Party") from and against any losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of third-party claims to the extent caused by (a) the Indemnifying Party's breach of this Agreement, or (b) the Indemnifying Party's gross negligence or willful misconduct. The Indemnified Party shall provide prompt written notice of any claim and cooperate reasonably in the defense. The Indemnifying Party shall control the defense and settlement of such claim, provided that no settlement that imposes liability or injunctive relief on the Indemnified Party may be made without the Indemnified Party's prior written consent (not to be unreasonably withheld).

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM A PARTY'S GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, OR A PARTY'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (INCLUDING LOST PROFITS OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION) EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. THE TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY OF EITHER PARTY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNTS PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

### Independent Contractor; Taxes

*Type: standard*

Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent or partner of Client. Freelancer shall be solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, and other statutory or contractual obligations of any sort, including workers' compensation insurance, if applicable. Client shall not control the means or methods by which Freelancer performs the services, except as expressly provided in this Agreement.

### Insurance (Optional)

*Type: optional*

Freelancer is encouraged to maintain professional liability and general liability insurance appropriate for Freelancer's business. If Client requires proof of insurance, Client shall request such proof in writing and the Parties shall meet to agree on scope and cost allocation. Unless otherwise agreed in writing, no specific insurance coverage is required under this Agreement.

### Publicity

*Type: negotiable*

Freelancer may include Client's name and the Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio, website, social media, and promotional materials, provided that such use does not disclose Client's Confidential Information or proprietary information. Client grants Freelancer a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to show the Deliverables for the foregoing promotional use. Client may require removal of specific portfolio items upon reasonable written request and for reasonable cause.

### Dispute Resolution; Governing Law

*Type: standard*

The Parties shall attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through negotiation between executives who have authority to settle. If the dispute is not resolved by negotiation within thirty (30) days, the Parties agree to participate in non-binding mediation in California before resorting to litigation. If mediation does not resolve the dispute, any legal action shall be brought exclusively in the state or federal courts located in the County of San Francisco, California. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of laws principles.

### Force Majeure

*Type: standard*

Neither Party shall be liable for any delay or failure in performance under this Agreement (except for payment obligations) to the extent such delay or failure is caused by events beyond the reasonable control of the affected Party, including acts of God, labor disputes, pandemics, governmental actions, acts of terrorism, or severe weather. The affected Party shall promptly notify the other Party of such event and shall use commercially reasonable efforts to resume performance.

### Notices

*Type: standard*

All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand, overnight courier, certified mail (return receipt requested), or email with confirmation of receipt to the addresses set forth below or to such other address as either Party may designate in writing. Notice is effective upon receipt.

### Severability; Entire Agreement; Amendments

*Type: standard*

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. This Agreement (including any exhibits or attachments) constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, and representations. Any amendment or modification to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by both Parties.

### Waiver; Assignment

*Type: standard*

No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver of that right. Neither Party may assign its rights or delegate its obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that Client may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets provided that the assignee assumes Client's obligations under this Agreement.

### Taxes and Expenses

*Type: standard*

Client shall be responsible for all sales, use, value added, or similar taxes (other than Freelancer's income taxes) arising from this Agreement. Any project-related expenses (stock imagery, licensed fonts, third-party plug-ins, courier costs, etc.) will be pre-approved by Client in writing and reimbursed upon submission of receipts or invoices unless otherwise included in the agreed fee.

### Portfolio and Credit

*Type: negotiable*

Freelancer shall have the right to include the work performed under this Agreement in Freelancer's professional portfolio and to attribute design credit to Freelancer and/or Chen Creative Studio on deliverables or marketing materials. Client may require removal of portfolio items if they contain sensitive or Confidential Information, in which case Client will identify those items and the Parties will work in good faith to accommodate Client's concerns.
